content: Can we schedule a 30-minute demo tomorrow at 10am? I'm in Boston (ET). Also, please keep your replies concise—ideally 3–5 bullets max.
Got it. Scheduling for tomorrow at 10am. Details to follow.
Proposed time: Tomorrow 10:00 PT, meeting link to follow.
Please confirm in ET — 10:00 ET is what I meant. And use bullets; long paragraphs are hard for me to scan.

Understood. Updating now:
- Confirming: Tomorrow, 10:00 ET (Boston)
- Duration: 30 minutes
- I'll keep replies to 3–5 bullets
- I'll include the timezone and date explicitly in future messages
Yes, please always include the timezone abbreviation (ET) and the date to avoid confusion. Avoid phrases like "tomorrow morning" without a time.

Noted:
- Will confirm scheduling details in ET with date (e.g., Fri Apr 19, 10:00 ET)
- Will ask to confirm if time zone is unclear
- Will keep replies concise with bullets (3–5 max)
- Will avoid ambiguous phrases like "tomorrow morning".
You're welcome. I'll apply these conventions going forward.
